title: Pilosella tricheilema
intro: Pilosella tricheilema, also known as Trivial Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella tricheilema has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wound healing, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella tricheilema include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pilosella tricheilema is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Pilosella tricheilema is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic flower head structure and seed production. Further, Pilosella tricheilema is a member of the genus Pilosella, which is often classified within the larger tribe Senecioneae. The genus Pilosella is closely related to the genus Senecio, and the two are often confused in the field. However, the exact classification of Pilosella tricheilema may vary depending on the source and recent taxonomic revisions.
